mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 148424.0 thousand, near historical highs
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k despite signal activation.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 2.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.69%, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS decline > 1% month-over-month) and housing starts (HOUST decline > 5% month-over-month) signals weakening consumer demand and forward economic contraction, producing negative 20-day equity returns as growth expectations fall.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 497559.0 million, HOUST at 1319.0 thousand
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth acceleration above 0.5% month-over-month signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of months where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.2% signals labor market cooling and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers negative 60-day equity returns as market participants anticipate earnings pressure from lower consumer spending and potential recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.9%, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 4.2%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth < 0.2%) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) signals real rate tightening that constrains equity valuations. This produces negative 20-day returns as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM growth < 0.2% AND FEDFUNDS > 1.5% simultaneously
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations. This produces positive 60-day equity returns as the market reprices growth and profit forecasts upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 17385.831 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 58% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward economic drag. Combined with retail sales (RSAFS) stagnation, this interaction indicates demand-side weakness producing negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1200 thousand AND RSAFS MoM growth < 0.1% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1319.0 thousand, above trigger
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or post-shock recovery phase
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.
